id: "19.1.30"
title: "Rack Server PSU N+1 Redundancy (Cisco UCS C-Series)"
criticality: "critical"
splunkPillar: "Observability"

# UC-19.1.30 · Rack Server PSU N+1 Redundancy (Cisco UCS C-Series)

## Description

This detection evaluates a 30m slice of `cisco:ucs:inventory` and `cisco:ucs:stats` where `classId` is one of `equipmentPsu`, `equipmentRackUnit`, or `equipmentPsuStats`, the `dn` is anchored to `sys/rack-unit-…` (PSU child paths `…/psu-M`), and per-slot `operState` is normalized with `coalesce()`. It joins `lookup ucs_rack_psu_baseline` on `rack_model` and emits `psu_operational_count`, `redundancy_delta`, and `severity_bucket` for rack 1+1 power margin—without using `equipmentChassisStats`, because UCSM does not publish a rack-level `psuRedundancy` enum like the blade `sys/chassis-…` path in UC-19.1.15.

## Value

On C-Series **rack servers** a two-PSU 1+1 design has little spare margin: losing one good supply is often a single point of failure to the next AC sag or RMA, while guests and apps keep running. Finance and app owners will not see that story in a VM or container log, but an unexpected loss of a second path can drop databases, vSphere clusters, or stand-alone line-of-business services that were already running warm. A rack-native table gives the NOC a `rack_unit_dn` to hand a data-center team before a second fault causes an outage, and it sits alongside UC-19.1.6 for kilowatt and fan heads-up—**margin** in one neighbour use case, **redundant supply posture** in this one, **blade layouts** in UC-19.1.15.

## Implementation

Enable **Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs** inventory and statistics inputs to the same UCSM. Place `lookups/ucs_rack_psu_baseline.csv` and `transforms.conf` for `ucs_rack_psu_baseline` on Search Heads. Run `| fieldsummary classId, dn, operState` on five live events, then save the `spl` as a 5m report or alert, throttle on `rack_unit_dn`, and require two consecutive non-`intact` bins before a page while your lookup is still maturing.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk add-on: **Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs** (Splunkbase 2731) on a Heavy Forwarder (or the tier that runs the UCS inventory and statistics modular inputs) and on Search Heads. A read-only UCSM account with rights to the rack and PSU managed objects. **Sourcetype truth:** internal wikis or older runbooks may say `cisco:ucs:environmental` — that is a documentation bug; the **Cisco UCS statistics modular input** (Splunk add-on) emits `cisco:ucs:stats` with a `classId` filter for `equipmentPsuStats` and related stat classes, and `cisco:ucs:inventory` for `equipmentRackUnit` and `equipmentPsu` on the `sys/rack-unit-…/psu-…` managed-object path when you test.
• **Field spelling:** the TA can flatten **XML** to `operState`, `oper_state`, or other variants. Before you put this search in production, run `index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" classId=equipmentPsu earliest=-1h | head 5` and add any extra spellings you see into the `coalesce()` list in a **local** saved-search copy—never guess field names the TA did not show you.
• **UC-19.1.6** is the kW, thermal, and fan programme: 24h statistics compared to a threshold CSV, status colours for environmental metrics, and a focus on *design margin* in watts and temperature bands. This UC, **19.1.30**, is the rack power-supply posture story for **C-Series** under `sys/rack-unit-…`: **what fraction of the installed PSUs are operable** on a 1+1 (typically two-bay) layout, because UCSM does **not** publish a `psuRedundancy` aggregate for rack objects the way `equipmentChassisStats` does for a blade chassis in **UC-19.1.15**. UC-19.1.15 uses `sys/chassis-…/psu-…`, `equipmentChassis`, and `equipmentChassisStats.psuRedundancy`; 19.1.30 uses `sys/rack-unit-…/psu-…`, `equipmentRackUnit`, and a **count-based** `redundancy_delta` because there is no UCSM `psuRedundancy` string to join for rack units. Keep 19.1.6 panels for margin; 19.1.15 for 5108/X-chassis; 19.1.30 for C220/C240/C480 service-profile **rack** domains.
• Network and RBAC: HTTPS to the UCSM VIP on `443`, CA bundle on the collection host, no interactive admin in the same account as the modular input. Index role includes `cisco_ucs` (or your org index).

**Step 1 — Configure data collection and the lookup**
(1) In Splunk Web, under **Settings → Data inputs** (wording can vary by build), open the Cisco UCS Inventory and Statistics input families, point them at the same `https://<ucsm-fqdn>`, the same `index` (commonly `cisco_ucs`), and ensure both streams are `disabled=0` after credentials are stored in Splunk (never paste secrets in chat or tickets). The inventory stream should land as `cisco:ucs:inventory`; statistics as `cisco:ucs:stats`.
(2) After two full poll periods, run `index=cisco_ucs earliest=-1h | stats count by sourcetype, classId` and confirm rows that include at least `equipmentRackUnit` and `equipmentPsu` on the inventory side, and `equipmentPsuStats` on the statistics side. If a class is missing, the enabled class list in the add-on is too small—add the class per Cisco guidance for that UCSM release, wait one more poll, re-check.
(3) File-based configuration (if your org prefers `inputs.conf` over the UI) lives under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/local/inputs.conf`. Copy stanza *names* and required keys from `default/inputs.conf` in the same app version; do not invent keys the vendor has not defined. A **literal** illustration of the shape you are aiming for, with placeholders only where the vendor’s default already uses a placeholder token, is:

```ini
# Copy vendor default stanzas; these lines are a structural guide only — verify against Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs 4.3.x default/inputs.conf on disk.
# [cisco_ucs://prod_ucs_inventory]
# index = cisco_ucs
# <follow vendor connection keys>
# disabled = 0

# [cisco_ucs://prod_ucs_stats]
# index = cisco_ucs
# <class_list must include at least equipmentPsuStats for per-PSU stat rows; expand for your org>
# disabled = 0
```

(4) On the Search Head, create a governed app (for example `cisco_ucs_ops`) and add `lookups/ucs_rack_psu_baseline.csv` plus a transform. File: `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/cisco_ucs_ops/local/transforms.conf`:

```ini
[ucs_rack_psu_baseline]
filename = ucs_rack_psu_baseline.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 1
default_match =
max_matches = 1
```

(5) The **literal** CSV body for the rack baseline (edit model strings to match *your* `equipmentRackUnit.model` emissions):

```csv
rack_model,default_psu_count,redundancy_intent,ac_or_dc,notes
UCS C220 M7,2,1+1,ac,C220 two-bay 1+1; tighten only after five sample events prove the model string
UCS C220 M6,2,1+1,ac,Same family as M7 for posture; not a thermal threshold row
UCS C240 M7,2,1+1,ac,C240 rack with redundant pair in production configs
UCS C240 M6,2,1+1,ac,Alias for sites that log M6 and M7 in the same row family
UCS C480 M5,2,1+1,ac,Four-socket C480 with a two-PSU redundant option per Cisco BOM
UCS-C220-M6,2,1+1,ac,If the MO emits a hyphenated product string instead of a space after UCS
C220-M7-SFF,2,1+1,ac,Shorter catalog token — confirm against inventory before you trust a single row
NON_REDUND_C_LAB,1,single,ac,Deliberate single-feed lab — expect info posture, not a midnight page
```

(6) Export or permission the lookup the same way you did for UC-19.1.6’s threshold table so the alert runner role can `| inputlookup ucs_rack_psu_baseline` in ad-hoc search.

**Step 2 — Build the saved search, understand the `dn` paths, and why counts replace `psuRedundancy` here**
(1) Paste the `spl` from this use case. The search limits to rack DNs: `sys/rack-unit-` not `sys/chassis-`, which is how you avoid re-implementing UC-19.1.15 by accident.
(2) `classId` is lower-cased into `cidl` so `equipmentPsu` and `equipmentpsu` both match. The **double `rex`**: the first hands you `rack_unit_dn` = `sys/rack-unit-N` for *every* relevant row; the second extracts `psu_bay` when the path continues with `/psu-M`.
(3) `is_oper` counts operable PSUs. The string list in the `if()` block is a pragmatic superset: add your site’s `fieldsummary` output if you see different enumerations. If `equipmentPsuStats` events lack `operState` at your version, the sum still includes the inventory `equipmentPsu` rows, which is the authority for FRU state.
(4) `psu_slot_rows` is the number of `equipmentPsu` inventory children seen for the rack. When a bay is empty or un-discovered for a cycle, the total can read low; the lookup `default_psu_count` provides the expected 1+1 size when inventory is momentarily partial.
(5) `redundancy_delta` is a state machine from counts and the lookup’s `redundancy_intent`, *not* a UCSM aggregate: there is no `psuRedundancy` for rack in the way `equipmentChassisStats` exposes for blades. `intact` means both bays show operable for a 1+1 intent, or the intent in the lookup says the design is not redundant and you have acknowledged that in `info` with `non_redundant_single_psu_by_design_or_inventory_gap` for single-supply or odd inventory. Tuning belongs in the CSV and in the `case()` order, not a silent loosening of hardware thresholds in UC-19.1.6.

**Step 3 — Validate against Cisco UCS Manager and the data hall**
(1) Pick one `rack_unit_dn` from the result table, open **Equipment → Rack Units** in the UCSM GUI, expand PSUs under that rack, and count operable vs inoperable for each `psu-` child; the counts should map to the table within one poll of the add-on’s cycle.
(2) If the model string in Splunk is null but the GUI has a nameplate, open the **XML** object for `equipmentRackUnit` in the API browser and read the `model` attribute; add that exact string as a new row in the CSV, `splunk restart` the SH if you version-control configs that way, and re-run the search.
(3) For dual-PDU or A/B feed work, compare with the facility **BMS**: UCSM can still show `operable` on both cards while a rack-side breaker is open by design. This UC does not replace an **electrical LOTO** record—treat contradiction longer than a poll as a human chase, not an automatic RMA of a good PSU.
(4) If inventory and stats disagree on whether a `psu-` `dn` exists, compare `_time` on both sourcetypes and check HTTPS trust on the statistics input after a UCSM certificate roll.
(5) Copy five raw events: `| search rack_unit_dn="sys/rack-unit-3"` with `| table _time, sourcetype, classId, dn, operState` to document field spellings in your Confluence for the next admin.

**Step 4 — Operationalize — dashboard row, schedule, and RACI**
(1) On the same dashboard tab you already use for UC-19.1.6, place a row 2 (or 3) `table` of `rack_unit_dn`, `rack_model`, `psu_operational_count`, `psu_total_count`, `redundancy_delta`, and `severity_bucket` so operators see both kW/thermal (row that belongs to 19.1.6) and 1+1 posture in one glance. Do not remove the 19.1.6 row—margin and redundant supplies are different failure modes.
(2) Alert schedule: every 5 minutes, `earliest = -35m` and `latest = now` to cover **inventory lag**. Fire when `redundancy_delta` is not `intact` and `severity_bucket` is `warning` or `critical`, with a **two-failure** requirement for the first month after you deploy the lookup.
(3) **RACI** (example): the compute platform team is accountable for the saved search and the CSV; data center operations is consulted for any dual-feed or breaker story; the NOC receives the `rack_unit_dn` in the page body. IaC owners are informed if the event correlates to a service-profile migration.
(4) **Pager routing**: map `degraded_rack_1_of_1plus1` to a short bridge if the org treats C-Series as tier-0; keep `info` and `non_redundant_single_psu…` to email for labs unless your policy says otherwise.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting and tuning (C-series rack specifics)**
• All rows `unknown_rack_model` — the `equipmentRackUnit` model you emit does not match any CSV `rack_model` string; fix the lookup first, not the `expect_psu` math.
• `psu_operational_count` bounces 1↔2 every poll — likely mixed `oper` spellings in one sourcetype; extend `coalesce()` and re-sample five events.
• Good hall power but a persistent `inoperable` on one supply — a bad line cord, a stuck ejector, or a BMC reseat in progress; walk the rack with DC ops before a same-day FRU.
• Both supplies `operable` but one feed dead at the bus — a BMS false green; do not call this a Splunk false positive—close the electrical ticket first, then re-check.
• C480 with three or four power options on paper but two bays in your build — trust inventory and the BOM row in the lookup, not a generic data sheet. If a future chassis moves to N+N with four supplies, you must add `default_psu_count=4` and rewrite the `case()` to match a four-way layout—do not copy blade 5108 rows from 19.1.15.
• Stats input disabled with rich inventory: `equipmentPsuStats` rows disappear; posture still works on inventory `operState` in most builds, but you lose cross-checks—**re-enable** stats and fix **TLS**, not a permanent blind spot.
• UCSM org migration renumbers rack units: expect a 15m spike in `unknown_rack_model` while templates repopulate; freeze alert severity during a tagged window.
• Intersight-primary C-series: per exclusions, do not run this as your only gate—add the Intersight stream to Splunk in parallel, or keep this search for legacy UCSM-only rows tagged by `host`.
• HX nodes: power posture is a different product; use HX-specific UCs, not a UCSM `rack-unit` `dn` search.
• Stretched cluster tests that power-down one side for hours: a controlled test should carry a **ServiceNow** change number that maps to a suppression lookup keyed on `rack_unit_dn` to avoid a weekend of redundant pages when both PSUs are actually healthy in UCSM.
• **C-series** vs **blade** confusion in the NOC — if a technician opens **chassis-PSU** in the GUI, hand them a printed diff: 19.1.15 = `sys/chassis-…/psu-…` with `equipmentChassisStats.psuRedundancy`; 19.1.30 = `sys/rack-unit-…/psu-…` with a count-based `redundancy_delta` and this rack baseline CSV. Mis-navigation is a common L1 time sink—fix with a one-picture diagram, not a looser **`case()`** that hides real 1+1 loss.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_ucs (sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" OR sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats") earliest=-30m@m
/* UC-19.1.30 — C-Series rack PSUs under sys/rack-unit-…/psu-…; no equipmentChassisStats.psuRedundancy for rack units; contrast UC-19.1.15 (blade chassis) */
| eval cidl=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, ""))
| where (cidl="equipmentpsu" OR cidl="equipmentrackunit" OR cidl="equipmentpsustats") AND isnotnull(dn) AND match(dn, "sys/rack-unit-")
| rex field=dn max_match=0 "^(?<rack_unit_dn>sys/rack-unit-[^/]+)(/|$)"
| rex field=dn max_match=0 "sys/rack-unit-(?P<rack_n>[^/]+)/psu-(?P<psu_bay>[^/]+)(/|$)"
| where isnotnull(rack_unit_dn) AND (cidl!="equipmentpsu" OR match(dn, "/psu-")) AND (cidl!="equipmentpsustats" OR match(dn, "/psu-"))
| eval oper=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(operState, oper_state, operstate, operability, ""))))
| eval is_oper=if(cidl="equipmentpsu" AND match(dn, "/psu-"), if(oper="operable" OR oper="operational" OR oper="ok" OR oper="online" OR oper="equipped",1,0), 0)
| eval is_psu_inv=if(cidl="equipmentpsu",1,0)
| eval rack_model_t=if(cidl="equipmentrackunit", toString(coalesce(model, mdl, modelStr, productName, product_name, mfgProductName, "")), null())
| sort 0 rack_unit_dn, dn, cidl, -_time
| dedup rack_unit_dn, dn, cidl
| sort 0 rack_unit_dn, -_time, cidl, dn
| stats sum(is_oper) as psu_operational_count, sum(is_psu_inv) as psu_slot_rows, values(dn) as psu_dns, latest(rack_model_t) as rack_model, latest(oper) as last_oper_sample by rack_unit_dn
| eval rack_model=if(rack_model=="" OR isnull(rack_model), null(), rack_model)
| lookup ucs_rack_psu_baseline rack_model OUTPUT default_psu_count, redundancy_intent, ac_or_dc, notes as baseline_notes
| fillnull value="" baseline_notes, ac_or_dc, redundancy_intent, rack_model
| eval default_psu_count=tonumber(coalesce(default_psu_count, "2"))
| eval expect_psu=if(isnotnull(default_psu_count) AND default_psu_count>0, default_psu_count, 2)
| eval default_psu_count=if(default_psu_count==0 OR isnull(default_psu_count), 2, default_psu_count)
| eval psu_total_count=if(psu_slot_rows>0, psu_slot_rows, expect_psu)
| eval redundancy_delta=case(
    (rack_model=="" OR isnull(rack_model)), "unknown_rack_model",
    psu_operational_count=0, "imminent_power_loss",
    psu_operational_count=1 AND psu_total_count>=2, "degraded_rack_1_of_1plus1",
    psu_operational_count<psu_total_count AND psu_operational_count>0 AND psu_total_count>=2, "degraded_rack_1_of_1plus1",
    psu_operational_count=psu_total_count AND psu_operational_count>=2 AND psu_total_count>=2, "intact",
    psu_operational_count=psu_total_count AND psu_total_count=1, "non_redundant_single_psu_by_design_or_inventory_gap",
    1=1, "at_risk_margins_check_lookup"
  )
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    redundancy_delta=="imminent_power_loss", "critical",
    (redundancy_delta="degraded_rack_1_of_1plus1" OR redundancy_delta="at_risk_margins_check_lookup" OR redundancy_delta="unknown_rack_model"), "warning",
    redundancy_delta=="intact", "ok",
    redundancy_delta=="non_redundant_single_psu_by_design_or_inventory_gap", "info",
    1=1, "warning"
  )
| table rack_unit_dn, rack_model, expect_psu, psu_total_count, psu_operational_count, redundancy_intent, redundancy_delta, severity_bucket, baseline_notes, last_oper_sample, psu_dns
| sort 0 severity_bucket, rack_unit_dn
```

## Visualization

A sortable `table` of `rack_unit_dn`, `rack_model`, `psu_operational_count`, and `redundancy_delta` with `rangemap` on `severity_bucket` (ok/warning/critical). A `single value` of rack units not in `intact` or in `info` single-PSU rows you plan to triage. A horizontal **bar** or **column** chart of `psu_operational_count` by `rack_unit_dn` to spot 1-of-2 states. A **line** `timechart` clone (15m) of `psu_operational_count` for a short RMA or dual-feed work window, not 24h environmental drift.
